title: Join preload threads binding via preload: [assoc: binding]
prompt: Write `MyApp.Blog.posts_with_category_loaded/0` that joins posts with their category in a single round trip. You MUST thread the join binding into the preload: `join: c in assoc(p, :category), preload: [category: c]`. Merely writing `preload: :category` without the binding would cause Ecto to issue a second query for categories. Write to `lib/my_app/blog.ex`.
tier_rationale: Single-query preload requires threading the binding — Claude often writes the join AND the preload but forgets to wire them together, causing a silent 2x.
research_citation: Research on Ecto preloads: 'Using preload with a join but not passing the bound variable via preload: [posts: p], causing Ecto to re-query the associations despite the join'
